{"notes": [{"url": "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40826125/", "title": "The effect of intermittent fasting on insulin resistance, lipid profile ...", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: page appears to be paywall/captcha/shell.", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/nutrition/articles/10.3389/fnut.2025.1664811/full", "title": "Intermittent fasting improves metabolic outcomes in ... - Frontiers", "summary": "Abstract Background: Previous studies have demonstrated that intermittent fasting (IF) has garnered scientific attention and gained recognition for its beneficial effects on metabolic outcomes. Accordingly, this systematic review and meta-analysis aimed to evaluate the effect of fasting on glycemic control, lipid profile, and inflammatory markers. Methods: Databases such as PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science were used to retrieve relevant studies published until September 2025.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Methods: Databases such as PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science were used to retrieve relevant studies published until September 2025.", "Moreover, the regimen successfully exerted its beneficial effect on low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C) (SMD = −0.34; 95% CI: −0.53, −0.14; p = 0.001) and interleukin-6 (IL-6) (SMD = −0.30; 95% CI: −0.57, −0.03; p = 0.029) levels as well.", "No evidence of publication bias was detected using Egger’s and Begg’s test (p > 0.05).", "In addition, the study protocol was approved by the International Prospective Register of Systematic Reviews (CRD420251142741). 2.1 Search strategy The PubMed, Scopus, Embase, Web of Science, and Cochrane Library were used to search for the relevant ", "The search was conducted from its inception through September 2025."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Methods: Databases such as PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science were used to retrieve relevant studies published until Septe"}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2589537024000981", "title": "Intermittent fasting and health outcomes: an umbrella review of ...", "summary": "Harris, L. ∙ McGarty, A. ∙ Hutchison, L. ∙ et al. Short-term intermittent energy restriction interventions for weight management: a systematic review and meta-analysis Obes Rev. 2017; 19(1):1-13 | Adults with overweight or obesity | 5 | 21–69 | 317 | IER | 14–48 | CER | Body weight | | Harris et al., 201852 52. Harris, L. ∙ Hamilton, S. ∙ Azevedo, L.B. ∙ et al.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Short-term intermittent energy restriction interventions for weight management: a systematic review and meta-analysis Obes Rev. 2017; 19(1):1-13 | Adults with overweight or obesity | 5 | 21–69 | 317 | IER | 14–48 | CER | Body weight | | Harris et al.", "Intermittent fasting interventions for treatment of overweight and obesity in adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis JBI Database System Rev Implement Rep. 2018; 16(2):507-547 | Adults with overweight or obesity | 4 | 40–50 | 287 | TWF, MADF |", "Health effects of alternate-day fasting in adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis Front Nutr. 2020; 7, 586036 | Adults with overweight or obesity | 7 | 18–70 | 269 | ADF | 4–48 | RD | Body weight, BMI, Total calorie intake, TC, TG, LDL-C, HDL-", "Intermittent versus continuous energy restriction for weight loss and metabolic improvement: a meta-analysis and systematic review Obesity (Silver Spring). 2021; 29(1):108-115 | Adults with overweight or obesity, T2DM, metabolic syndrome | 11 | 28–71", "A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials Crit Rev Food Sci Nutr. 2021; 63(15):2331-2347 | Adults with overweight or obesity | 6 | 18–65 | 348 | TRE | 6–48 | Habitual diet | Body weight (%), BMI, Lean mass, Visceral fat ma"], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Short-term intermittent energy restriction interventions for weight management: a systematic review and meta-analysis Obes Rev. 2017; 19(1):"}, {"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11559166/", "title": "Effects of different types of intermittent fasting on metabolic outcomes", "summary": "Abstract Background Intermittent fasting (IF) holds promise for enhancing metabolic health. However, the optimum IF forms and their superiority over continuous energy restriction (CER) remain unclear due to disconnected findings. Methods We systematically searched PubMed, Embase, and the Cochrane databases for meta-analyses of randomized controlled trials (RCTs) investigating the association between IF and metabolic health outcomes.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Subsequently, we performed an umbrella review and network meta-analysis (NMA) to evaluate the efficacy of different forms of IF (time-restricted eating (TRE), alternate-day fasting (ADF), and 5:2 diet (regular eating for 5 days and energy restriction", "Results Ten of the best and non-redundant meta-analysis studies, involving 153 original studies and 9846 participants, were included.", "In the SUCRA of NMA, IF ranked higher than usual diets or CER in 85.4% and 56.1% of the outcomes, respectively.", "ADF had the highest overall ranking for improving metabolic health (ranked first: 64.3%, ranked second: 14.3%).", "Supplementary Information The online version contains supplementary material available at 10.1186/s12916-024-03716-1."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Subsequently, we performed an umbrella review and network meta-analysis (NMA) to evaluate the efficacy of different forms of IF (time-restri"}, {"url": "https://www.cureus.com/articles/304835-the-role-of-intermittent-fasting-on-metabolic-syndrome-a-systematic-review-and-meta-analysis", "title": "The Role of Intermittent Fasting on Metabolic Syndrome", "summary": "Abstract Intermittent fasting has gained popularity as a dietary intervention to improve metabolic health. Metabolic syndrome may benefit from intermittent fasting by improving weight, cholesterol levels, blood pressure (BP), and glucose control. This study aims to assess the effects of intermittent fasting on weight, BMI, cholesterol levels, BP, and glucose in individuals with metabolic syndrome.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Analysis showed significant improvements in weight, with a mean reduction of 3.59 kg (95% CI: -4.59 to -2.59, p < 0.0001) and a decrease in BMI of 1.39 kg/m2 (95% CI: -1.87 to -0.92, p < 0.0001).", "Low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol levels dropped by 56.22 mg/dL (95% CI: -80.14 to -32.29, p < 0.0001), and systolic BP decreased by 5.54 mmHg (95% CI: -7.55 to -3.53, p < 0.0001).", "These risk factors include elevated blood pressure (BP), high blood sugar, excess body fat around the waist, and abnormal cholesterol or triglyceride levels [2].", "The fasting periods vary in length depending on the method, with some protocols involving fasting for 16 hours a day (such as the 16:8 method) or fasting for 24 hours once or twice a week (as seen in the 5:2 method) [6].", "This leads to a reduction in body fat and weight, which can help manage or even reverse conditions associated with metabolic syndrome [8]."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Analysis showed significant improvements in weight, with a mean reduction of 3.59 kg (95% CI: -4.59 to -2.59, p < 0.0001) and a decrease in "}, {"url": "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/398583827_Intermittent_fasting_improves_metabolic_outcomes_in_metabolic_syndrome_a_systematic_review_and_meta-analysis_with_GRADE_evaluation", "title": "a systematic review and meta-analysis with GRADE evaluation", "summary": "# Security check required We've detected unusual activity from your network. 2.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["To continue, complete the security check below. 驗證成功。正在等待 www.researchgate.net 回應 Ray ID: 9ece154c1d5b1c43 Client IP: 2001:569:73b9:3500:c112:d997:22e9:c45a © 2008-2026 ResearchGate GmbH.", "A 300-400 word deep analysis focused on findings relevant to the research question, including:
